category_gap_analysis.current_candidate_status: candidate-0031 through candidate-0034 provide four Codex-suggested core examples, pending manual confirmation.
category_gap_analysis.recommendation: Do not collect more for this category before human review unless final confirmation rejects one of these candidates.

category_gap_analysis.current_candidate_status: candidate-0036 and candidate-0037 are the only supplemental hardcoded_behavior candidates currently recommended for the core pool; candidate-0035 is retained as secondary_research because the source proves a workflow hardcoding defect but not enough Agent-authored target-project delivery evidence.
category_gap_analysis.recommendation: Keep candidate-0035 as secondary research and count only candidate-0036 and candidate-0037 toward the core hardcoded_behavior distribution; collect exactly one additional true core candidate before final confirmation if the 20-case M0 gate remains required.

category_gap_analysis.current_candidate_status: candidate-0038 and candidate-0039 provide two Codex-suggested core examples with build/static success plus runtime-path failure evidence, pending manual confirmation.
category_gap_analysis.recommendation: Keep these for manual final review; candidate-0039 should receive extra boundary review because the runtime failure is an unreachable optimization path rather than a crash.

notes: Strict scope correction moves candidate-0035 to secondary research. The audit now has 19 Codex-suggested core candidates and remains short by 1; it does not approve, reproduce, source-verify, reject, delete, or migrate any candidate.

scope_definitions.core_in_scope: Directly relevant to VeriCrate first-version failure research and eligible for final human review if source, deduplication, privacy, and reproduction checks pass.
scope_definitions.secondary_research: Relevant to Agent/tool reliability or future design, but not countable toward M0 formal cases for the first local MVP.
scope_definitions.out_of_scope: Excluded from the first formal case pool unless new evidence ties it to an Agent code-delivery failure detectable by VeriCrate.

records.candidate_title: One-time local automation stayed ACTIVE but did not run at scheduled time
records.scope_reason: The source describes a local automation scheduler state bug, not an Agent code-delivery result. It does not involve target project files, tests, API, database, or a verifiable completion claim.

records.candidate_title: VS Code queue repeatedly executes completed task instead of advancing to the next queued item
records.scope_reason: The queue repeats or stalls tasks, which is useful orchestration research, but the source does not tie the failure to an independently checkable project delivery artifact.

records.candidate_title: Automations using Worktrees do not run Setup script
records.scope_reason: Worktree automation skipping setup can inform Runner input design, but it is an execution-environment/setup failure rather than an Agent delivering incorrect project code.

records.candidate_title: App-server process fails with ENOENT due to incorrect binary path (codex/codex vs bin/codex) on macOS ARM64
records.scope_reason: The failure is a packaged app-server binary path problem after installation. It is a client/package bug, not a target repository delivery failure.

records.candidate_title: Misleading Browser policy error when configured Codex CLI path does not exist
records.scope_reason: The issue concerns client error mapping for a missing CLI path and Browser policy messaging, not an Agent-generated project change.

records.candidate_title: Windows setup fails before the UAC prompt, and both setup options are non-functional
records.scope_reason: The issue is a Windows first-run/UAC setup flow bug. It does not involve code generated or modified for a target repository.

records.candidate_title: [Feature Request] Improve handling of usage limits during plan implementation with agent state preservation
records.scope_reason: The Agent claimed a plan was complete while expected files were missing. Git/file checks and existing tests can independently challenge the completion claim.

records.candidate_title: [Bug] Agent chains `git stash pop` after failed `git stash push`, corrupting unrelated user stash
records.scope_reason: The Agent executed a dangerous Git workflow and damaged unrelated stash/worktree state while claiming clean completion. This is central to independent evidence review, though stash-specific checks exceed the first MVP baseline.

records.candidate_title: permissions.deny + PreToolUse hook did not block a Task-tool subagent from reading a denied file (blocked correctly on retest)
records.scope_reason: The report is about the Agent product permission boundary for subagents, not code delivered into an application repository. It is relevant to security research but not a first-pool product delivery case.

records.candidate_title: v2.1.204: failed self-update deletes live background agents' worktrees; completed agents can't be removed (Ctrl+X no-op) and reappear after restart
records.scope_reason: The source describes an updater/session/worktree data-loss incident. It is serious, but the root is client lifecycle behavior rather than task-specific code delivery.

records.candidate_title: [BUG] Agent view: Ctrl+X delete on Completed sessions doesn't persist — rows reappear at bottom of Completed; sessions double-rendered (2.1.204)
records.scope_reason: The issue is a TUI completed-session list persistence bug. It does not validate an Agent task result in a target project.

records.candidate_title: [BUG] Subprocess initialization did not complete within 60000ms on Windows — regression from 2.1.114
records.scope_reason: The issue is a Windows client update/subprocess startup regression. It is excluded unless tied to a concrete erroneous project delivery, which this source does not show.

records.candidate_title: LLM did not conform to the edit | SEARCH/REPLACE block failed to match!
records.scope_reason: Aider planned or described a code edit but failed to apply it. Git Diff, file hashes, and tests can directly show whether the requested change exists.

records.candidate_title: Gemini 2.5 Pro through aistudio: Empty response received from LLM. Check your provider account?
records.scope_reason: The issue centers on intermittent empty model/provider responses. Without a delivered project artifact or false completion, it falls under provider/tool reliability rather than VeriCrate first-pool delivery failure.

records.candidate_title: Built-in scraping path attempts cloud metadata endpoints with no private-IP or metadata guardrails
records.scope_reason: The Agent/tool path attempted a dangerous metadata URL and generated unconstrained request code. It maps to first-version security-boundary and configuration evidence, but requires careful sandboxed reproduction.

records.candidate_title: Uncaught exception when using --user-input-color with wrong format
records.scope_reason: The issue is ordinary CLI parameter validation for Aider startup, not an Agent code-delivery or completion claim failure.

records.candidate_title: Bug report: When using Aider with a local Ollama model the model is unable to see any files added to the chat
records.scope_reason: The source was closed as a duplicate and comments show files were sent to the model. The remaining issue is local model/context configuration, not a verified Agent delivery failure.

records.candidate_title: Unable to use diff editing with local instance of qwen2.5-coder-32b-instruct
records.scope_reason: The local model could not reliably perform diff editing, which is useful compatibility research, but the root is model/config capability rather than a completed project delivery artifact.

records.candidate_title: Cline does not wait for terminal command to finish
records.scope_reason: The Agent declared a command/task complete after a timeout without evidence. Exit codes, build/test output, and repeated command observation can independently verify the claim.

records.candidate_title: New Update 4.0.1 the agents keeps loosing its focus on the task .
records.scope_reason: The Agent lost task focus after resume and emitted Task Completed despite the repair target not being satisfied. Diff and tests can check the actual delivery.

records.candidate_title: Claude incorrectly recommended removing Prisma multiSchema directives, causing runtime database errors
records.scope_reason: The Agent recommended a project configuration change that caused runtime database failures. Database/API/runtime evidence can directly validate the bad delivery.

records.candidate_title: Bob generates incorrect comment format in go.mod files.
records.scope_reason: The Agent generated an invalid go.mod comment format. Even though this is build failure rather than build-passes-runtime-fails, build output and file checks make it a concrete delivery failure.

records.candidate_title: Spurious "result missing" / "[ERROR] You did not use a tool in your previous response!" despite a valid tool call (succeeds on identical retry)
records.scope_reason: The result-missing/tool-call issue is relevant to Agent reliability, but the candidate does not preserve a concrete target-project delivery result to verify.

records.candidate_title: team_run_task sync mode reports status "running" after the task has completed
records.scope_reason: The issue is an SDK/team task status inconsistency. It can be API-tested, but it is the Agent framework product API, not an application code delivery failure.

records.candidate_title: GitHub Copilot Chat thinks it's editing files but it's not making any changes and nothing gets updated.
records.scope_reason: Copilot Agent mode appeared to edit files but project files remained unchanged or empty. Git Diff and file hashes can verify the missing delivery.

records.candidate_title: Copilot agent mode keeps failing to create/edit files on the remote SSH host and creates/edits files on the local machine instead
records.scope_reason: The issue involves Remote SSH file writes to the wrong location and is a useful supplemental source, but M0-03 already recommends merging it with candidate-0029 and the trigger includes nondefault extension placement.

records.candidate_title: Configure Tools UI writes incorrect tool names with prefix notation, corrupting custom agent files
records.scope_reason: The source concerns a VS Code configuration UI corrupting custom agent files. It is a product/configuration bug rather than a coding task delivery failure.

records.candidate_title: Formatting errors with undo using agent mode
records.scope_reason: Rejecting or undoing Agent changes introduced unwanted code edits. Before/after Git evidence and file hashes make this a direct delivery-artifact integrity failure.

records.candidate_title: Agent mode cannot edit files with remote-ssh extension
records.scope_reason: Remote SSH file editing failures are environment-specific, but the core symptom is an Agent unable to modify target project files. Local MVP can partially model this as Git/file-output verification, not Remote SSH support.

records.candidate_title: Sorry, no response was returned (Agent mode, Gemini 2.5 Pro)
records.scope_reason: The source is an Agent-mode/model/tool-schema no-response failure. It lacks a delivered project artifact or independent completion claim suitable for first-pool VeriCrate validation.

records.candidate_title: Code agent replaced the only Service spec-drift test with a label-stripping test
records.scope_reason: The case is directly about an Agent code-delivery artifact changing tests so that the original behavior is no longer checked. Git diff plus mutation or existing-test evidence can independently show whether the replacement test detects Service spec-drift failures.

records.candidate_title: Code agent added SSL validator tests that exercised mock credentials but not protocol selection
records.scope_reason: The case is a target-project code-delivery failure where added tests do not validate the runtime path the Agent changed. Reverting the fix or mutating protocol selection while running the new tests can independently detect the weakness.

records.candidate_title: Code agent wrote a column-alignment test with equal values that left padding untested
records.scope_reason: The issue is directly tied to Agent-authored tests in a project artifact. A mutation check that removes padding, combined with the equal-value test data, can independently show the test was not detecting the target behavior.

records.candidate_title: Code agent used a dry-run success assertion that bypassed the auto-skip branch
records.scope_reason: This is a project-delivery test artifact that does not exercise the intended runtime branch. Existing tests plus a branch-coverage or mutation check can independently detect that dry-run bypasses the target logic.

records.candidate_title: Code-agent delivery workflow hardcoded TARGET_BRANCH to main
records.scope_reason: The source supports that the delivery workflow hardcoded the target branch to main. However, it does not sufficiently establish that this workflow code itself was generated as an AI coding Agent delivery artifact for the target project. Full validation would also depend on GitHub default-branch API behavior, PR creation flow, or a remote GitHub Actions environment. The case is therefore valuable secondary research for future VeriCrate GitHub workflow design, but should not count in the first M1-M3 local MVP core-case pool.

records.candidate_title: Code agent hardcoded an incomplete non-content file exclusion list
records.scope_reason: The case is an Agent-authored code artifact whose fixed list fails for an equivalent project file layout. File/config inspection and a fixture containing the omitted template can independently detect the hardcoding.

records.candidate_title: Code agent copied a fixed docs/plans path into generic scaffolded skills
records.scope_reason: The source connects an Agent PR to a project artifact containing fixed paths inappropriate for generic scaffolds. Git diff and template/file inspection can detect it, but full downstream impact needs human confirmation.

records.candidate_title: Claude Code-generated fallback passed Go checks but broke functional triage runs
records.scope_reason: This is a target-project delivery where the PR's Go vet and targeted tests passed but the real functional harness path failed after merge. Build logs plus functional-test logs can independently validate the mismatch.

records.candidate_title: Code-agent PR passed pre-commit and tests but shipped unreachable skopeo optimization
records.scope_reason: The case involves Agent-authored project code that passed checks but failed to deliver its runtime behavior in the deployment environment. A local MVP can check dependency availability and branch reachability, though exact GitHub runner reproduction may need environment modeling.
